name: My business showed up wrong in Google AI Overviews and I lost customers - can I sue Google?
description: Unlikely due to Section 230 immunity; document losses and seek tech liability counsel.

mainEntity.name: My business showed up wrong in Google AI Overviews and I lost customers - can I sue Google?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlikely due to Section 230 immunity; document losses and seek tech liability counsel.
acceptedAnswer.description: No, you likely cannot sue Google successfully for business losses from inaccurate AI Overviews, as Section 230 of the Communications Decency Act immunizes them from liability for user-generated content summaries.[1][4] Courts have not recognized viable claims against Google for AI hallucinations in search features, similar to past SEO disputes. Key factors: Prove direct causation between AI error and losses; Section 230 bars most claims; no precedent for AI Overview-specific damages. Document all lost revenue evidence immediately, consult a lawyer experienced in tech liability, and consider reputation management alternatives. Nationwide applicability, but state consumer laws may offer limited angles.
